prompt.content:
Create a new spreadsheet dedicated to differentiator criteria and switching likelihood based on respondent interest in the former mine. 

In the spreadsheet, create a table that reports the average switching likelihood (rounded to two decimal places) and the total respondent count (as whole numbers) for each of the five key differentiators: Lowest cost/pricing, High server connection/reliability, Robust cybersecurity, Best customer services, and Strongest public sector expertise. Use the latest survey data. Filter the data for those who are very interested or somewhat interested in a new mine, and present the data values for each of the two segments.

rubrics.name: States the average of switching likelihood for somewhat interested respondents... (ver_6446752a02cc47bf8555676d216c2b5e)

messages.content: States the average of switching likelihood for somewhat interested respondents is 4.00 (acceptable range 3.96 to 4.04).

rubrics.name: States the average of switching likelihood for somewhat interested is any... (ver_6446752a02cc47bf8555676d216c2b5e)

messages.content: States the average of switching likelihood for somewhat interested is any other number than 4.00.

rubrics.name: States the average of switching likelihood for very interested respondents is... (ver_cd34fe0904e34f4a9cdafda7f4d44740)

messages.content: States the average of switching likelihood for very interested respondents is 2.83 (acceptable range 2.80 to 2.86).

rubrics.name: States the average of switching likelihood for very interested is any other... (ver_cd34fe0904e34f4a9cdafda7f4d44740)

messages.content: States the average of switching likelihood for very interested is any other number than 2.83.
